guide.title: Syntax Guide
guide.subtitle: Quick overview of what a predicate-logic model is and how to work with this app.

overview.title: Global Overview
overview.text: A predicate-logic model describes a world using objects and relations. In this app, you build that world with three parts: universe, constants, and predicates.

universe.title: 1. Universe
universe.text: List all elements that can exist in the model domain.

constants.title: 2. Constants
constants.text: Map constant names used in formulas to universe elements.

predicates.title: 3. Predicates
predicates.text: Define facts and relations by using predicate names with arguments.

query.title: Query Field Syntax
query.whatItDoes: The query checks what follows from your model. It can return true or false, or bindings for variables (for example, which values satisfy ?x).

syntax.atom: Use atoms in the form Predicate(arg1, arg2, ...).
syntax.terms: Arguments can be constants (ann) or variables (?x).
syntax.logical: Supported logical operators: and, or, not, ->, <->.
syntax.quantifiers: Supported quantifiers: forAll (∀) ?x: ... and exists (∃) ?x: ... (Unicode forms are also supported).
syntax.grouping: Use parentheses to group expressions and control precedence.

visualization.title: Visualization
visualization.text: The visualization renders your model as a directed graph, based on resolved universe values from predicate facts.

items.nodes: Each universe element is shown as a node.
items.edges: Binary predicates create directed edges labeled by predicate name; unary predicates are shown as self-loops.
items.highlight: After query execution, nodes can show matched variable bindings.

visualization.exampleCaption: Example: nodes and labeled edges generated from predicate facts.

items.validation: Syntax and semantic validation for universe, constants, predicates and query.
items.visualization: Graph visualization of constants and predicate links.
items.queryExecution: Query execution with variables, logical operators and quantifiers (forAll, exists).
items.examples: Example presets to quickly load working models and queries.
items.results: Result inspection in both boolean and variable-binding form.
